title: MODEL VILLAGES AND RECOGNITION OF BEST-PERFORMING PANCHAYATS
text:
Press Release:Press Information Bureau

Ministry of Panchayati Raj

MODEL VILLAGES AND RECOGNITION OF BEST-PERFORMING PANCHAYATS

Posted On:
11 MAR 2026 2:38PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) has been incentivizing the best performing Panchayats under its scheme of Incentivization of Panchayats (IoP), one of the central components of Centrally Sponsored scheme of revamped Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA). The best performing Panchayats in the country are conferred with National Panchayat Awards and given financial incentives by organising competition at national level every year. These awards are currently aligned to Localisation of Sustainable Development Goals (LSDGs) to assess the performance of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in attainment of SDGs in the areas of poverty reduction and livelihood support, health, women and child development, water sufficiency, climate action and clean environment, infrastructure development, social security, and promotion of good governance in the Panchayats. Financial incentives in the range of Rs.50 lakh to Rs.5 crore is released to the best performing Panchayat depending on the Panchayat tier and category of Award.

A list of panchayats which have been conferred with the National Panchayat Award during the Award Years 2023, 2024 and 2025 is at
Annexure-I.

Besides, the Ministry compiles Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) based on well-defined key performance indicators relating to poverty reduction and livelihood support, women and child development, water sufficiency, climate action and clean environment, infrastructure development, social security, and promotion of good governance to measure the localisation of SDGs. The major key performance indicators under PAI are given at
Annexure-II
. The Panchayats are ranked based on their performance on the key performance indicators and described as Achievers (with PAI score of 90-100), Front Runners (with PAI score of 75-below 90), Performers (with PAI score of 60-below 75), Aspirants (with PAI score of 40-below 60), and Beginners (with PAI score of 0- below 40). The group-wise details of   Gram Panchayats in various States/UTs including Punjab are available on portal of the Ministry-PAI.GOV.IN.

Annexure-II



Key Performance Indicators for Compilation of Panchayat Advancement Index



Sl. No.

Theme

Theme-wise key PAI 2.0 indicators in brief

1

Poverty Free and Enhanced Livelihoods Panchayat

Social protection coverage, housing, livelihoods, pensions, SHGs, basic banking & disaster preparedness

2

Healthy Panchayat

Maternal & child health, nutrition outcomes, immunisation, health staffing & services availability

3

Child Friendly Panchayat

Early childhood care, schooling infrastructure, nutrition status, safety, education transition

4

Water Sufficient Panchayat

Drinking water access, water quality, ODF+ status, water budgeting, NRM & rainwater harvesting

5

Clean and Green Panchayat

Sanitation, waste management, renewable energy, plastic ban, environmental cleanliness

6

Self-sufficient Infrastructure in Panchayat

Physical & digital infrastructure, roads, public buildings, CSCs, community facilities

7

Socially Just and Socially Secured Panchayat

Inclusion under social protection schemes, social security, pensions, disability support, crime prevention & welfare access

8

Panchayat with Good Governance

Planning through Gram Panchayat Development Plans, Own Sources of Revenue mobilisation, transparency, digital governance, Gram Sabha functioning

9

Women Friendly Panchayat

Women’s health, education, SHGs, safety, participation, gender equity indicators



This information was given by Union Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh in a written reply in Rajya Sabha on 11
th
 March 2026.

Ministry of Panchayati Raj

MODEL VILLAGES AND RECOGNITION OF BEST-PERFORMING PANCHAYATS

Posted On:
11 MAR 2026 2:38PM by PIB Delhi

The Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) has been incentivizing the best performing Panchayats under its scheme of Incentivization of Panchayats (IoP), one of the central components of Centrally Sponsored scheme of revamped Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA). The best performing Panchayats in the country are conferred with National Panchayat Awards and given financial incentives by organising competition at national level every year. These awards are currently aligned to Localisation of Sustainable Development Goals (LSDGs) to assess the performance of Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in attainment of SDGs in the areas of poverty reduction and livelihood support, health, women and child development, water sufficiency, climate action and clean environment, infrastructure development, social security, and promotion of good governance in the Panchayats. Financial incentives in the range of Rs.50 lakh to Rs.5 crore is released to the best performing Panchayat depending on the Panchayat tier and category of Award.

A list of panchayats which have been conferred with the National Panchayat Award during the Award Years 2023, 2024 and 2025 is at
Annexure-I.

Besides, the Ministry compiles Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) based on well-defined key performance indicators relating to poverty reduction and livelihood support, women and child development, water sufficiency, climate action and clean environment, infrastructure development, social security, and promotion of good governance to measure the localisation of SDGs. The major key performance indicators under PAI are given at
Annexure-II
. The Panchayats are ranked based on their performance on the key performance indicators and described as Achievers (with PAI score of 90-100), Front Runners (with PAI score of 75-below 90), Performers (with PAI score of 60-below 75), Aspirants (with PAI score of 40-below 60), and Beginners (with PAI score of 0- below 40). The group-wise details of   Gram Panchayats in various States/UTs including Punjab are available on portal of the Ministry-PAI.GOV.IN.

Annexure-II



Key Performance Indicators for Compilation of Panchayat Advancement Index



Sl. No.

Theme

Theme-wise key PAI 2.0 indicators in brief

1

Poverty Free and Enhanced Livelihoods Panchayat

Social protection coverage, housing, livelihoods, pensions, SHGs, basic banking & disaster preparedness

2

Healthy Panchayat

Maternal & child health, nutrition outcomes, immunisation, health staffing & services availability

3

Child Friendly Panchayat

Early childhood care, schooling infrastructure, nutrition status, safety, education transition

4

Water Sufficient Panchayat

Drinking water access, water quality, ODF+ status, water budgeting, NRM & rainwater harvesting

5

Clean and Green Panchayat

Sanitation, waste management, renewable energy, plastic ban, environmental cleanliness

6

Self-sufficient Infrastructure in Panchayat

Physical & digital infrastructure, roads, public buildings, CSCs, community facilities

7

Socially Just and Socially Secured Panchayat

Inclusion under social protection schemes, social security, pensions, disability support, crime prevention & welfare access

8

Panchayat with Good Governance

Planning through Gram Panchayat Development Plans, Own Sources of Revenue mobilisation, transparency, digital governance, Gram Sabha functioning

9

Women Friendly Panchayat

Women’s health, education, SHGs, safety, participation, gender equity indicators



This information was given by Union Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh in a written reply in Rajya Sabha on 11
th
 March 2026.
